title: Triggers for Processing User Events
text: Triggers for Processing User Events In desktop applications, an end user can activate triggers by pressing a key, choosing a menu option, or invoking macros to request an action. If the context is appropriate, the related trigger is fired. For example, pressing Ctrl+S invokes the ^STORE macro, causing Uniface to perform any outstanding data validation checks. If any of these return an error, the store trigger is not activated. Table 1. Triggers Activated by Structure Editor Functions Trigger Level trigger keyboardSwitch Application trigger userKey Application and component trigger menu Application, component, entity, and field trigger accept trigger clear trigger erase trigger print trigger quit trigger retrieve trigger retrieveSequential trigger store Component trigger create trigger remove Entity trigger help trigger detail Entity and field trigger next trigger previous Field Was this topic helpful?
